q: Energy demand is the term used to describe the consumption of energy by human activities.
Energy demand drives the whole energy system, influencing the total amount of energy used.
Energy demand depends on socioeconomic factors such as net capital income and development of technologies.
Energy demand forecasting is only useful for identifying current prices and has no role in environmental security.
There has been an enormous increase in the global demand for energy in recent years due to industrial development.
At present, the supply of energy is much greater than the actual demand.
The population and new technology are always expanding, leading to a year by year increase in energy demand.
The energy crisis refers to the concern that limited natural resources are diminishing as demand rises.
An energy crisis is a situation in which a nation suffers from a disruption of energy supplies connected by increasing energy prices.
Approximately ninety percent of the world's oil reserves have already been discovered.
Common forms of energy in daily life include heat, light, and electricity.
A non-renewable resource is a natural substance that is replenished faster than it is consumed.
Non-renewable energy resources include coal, natural gas, crude oil, and nuclear energy.
Non-renewable resources occur in limited and exhaustible quantities.
Renewable energy sources are also called non-conventional energy sources.
Renewable energy sources are continuously replenished by natural processes.
Renewable energy can be produced at a rate greater than or equal to the rate of consumption.
Renewable resources take millions of years to be replenished.
Wind, solar, and hydro are examples of renewable energy sources.
Renewable energy sources have a virtual unlimited supply.
Carbon is the main constituent of fossil fuels.
Chemical energy stored in fossil fuels is converted into heat, light, and mechanical energy.
The fossil fuel industry extracts crude oil from the ground and converts it into gasoline.
Petrochemical products include plastics and polyurethane.
The process of coal formation is called coalification.
Coal is made of decomposed plant matter in conditions of high temperature and pressure.
Coal is a uniform substance with a constant composition from deposit to deposit.
Natural gas always contains methane.
Methane is a common gas found in swamps.
Nuclear energy is renewable because uranium is found in the Earth's crust.
Uranium fuels are consumed in the nuclear fission reaction.
Vessels driven by nuclear energy can sail for long periods without having to refuel.
Radioisotopes obtained as by-products in nuclear reactions are used in medicine and agriculture.
Earth's atmosphere and clouds absorb or scatter as much as $54 \%$ of the incoming sunlight.
The sunlight that reaches the ground consists of visible light, infrared, and ultraviolet radiation.
Irradiance is measured in $W \cdot m^{-2}$ or $kW \cdot m^{-2}$.
Insolation is the solar irradiance received on a horizontal surface such as ground.
The solar constant has an intensity of approximately $1350 W \cdot m^{-2}$ above the earth's atmosphere.
On a clear day at solar noon, solar radiation has an intensity of approximately $1000 W \cdot m^{-2}$ at the earth's surface.
The three main ways to harness solar energy are photovoltaic, solar heating, and concentrated solar power.
A solar cell is basically a p-n junction diode.
Power conversion efficiency is the ratio of solar power received to the electrical power delivered.
Solar heating systems in homes include a solar collector, insulated piping, and a hot water storage tank.
Flat plate collectors use copper pipes attached to an absorber plate.
Concentrated solar power systems use mirrors or lenses to concentrate sunlight.
The output of a wind turbine is proportional to the cube of the wind speed.
Wind power potential increases by a factor of eight when wind speed doubles.
Hydropower is one of the oldest and largest sources of renewable energy.
A penstock is an open canal that regulates water flow with gates and valves.
In a pumped storage facility, energy is stored by pumping water from a lower reservoir to an upper reservoir.
Tidal energy is produced by the gravitational pull of the moon on the spinning earth.
Ocean thermal-energy conversion plants exploit the temperature difference between surface and deep water.
Geothermal energy is taken from the Earth's core.
Biomass is renewable organic material that comes from plants and animals.
Direct combustion is the most common method for converting biomass to useful energy.
Biomass gasification produces synthesis gas, also known as syngas.
The biological conversion of biomass into ethanol involves fermentation.
Biogas or biomethane is produced in anaerobic digesters.
Environmental problems related to energy include air pollution, climate change, and water pollution.
Global warming refers to the gradual increase in the average temperature of the earth's surface.
Main greenhouse gases include carbon dioxide, methane, and chlorofluorocarbons.
CFCs help maintain the earth's protective ozone layer.
Acid rains are caused mainly by the release of sulphur dioxide ($SO_2$) and oxides of nitrogen ($NO_x$).
The oil industry adds lead to petrol to help engines run more smoothly.
Leaded fumes from vehicles increase intelligence in children.
Oil spills can damage the ecosystem and its habitats.
Excavated areas for coal mining can cause water pollution due to surface water runoff.
Photovoltaic systems make no noise and cause no pollution while in operation.
Modern wind turbine designs locate the blades downwind to reduce noise.
Infrasound from wind turbines is usually beneath the threshold of human hearing.
Wind turbines may create shadow flicker on nearby residences.
Negative impacts of dams include breeding grounds for mosquitoes and spreading of disease.
Geothermal power is considered a relatively benign source of energy.
Biomass power plants raise similar concerns about air emissions as fossil fuel plants.
In fossil fuel energy transfer, chemical energy is first converted into electrical energy.
Nuclear energy transfer involves converting nuclear energy to thermal energy, then kinetic, then electrical.
Wind energy transfer goes from kinetic energy directly to electrical energy.
Hydroelectricity energy transfer begins with gravitational potential energy.
